title: Quick Question About Episode 15
wikitext:
[]: Quick Question About Episode 15             Quote Post by Raian-kun » Thu Aug 06, 2009 3:04 am What piece is Shinji playing on the cello about 11 minutes into episode 15 (Those Women Longed for the Touch of Other's Lips, and Thus Invited Their Kisses), and who is it by? It's one of those classical pieces where if you aren't a big classical music buff, you "know" it as in you've heard it in other anime, films etc. before but don't know what it actually is called or who it's by. Any help here is appreciated.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Aug 06, 2009 3:08 am J.S. Bach - Cello Suite no. 1: Prelude It's a subtle example of character development as well since Shinji says "he's not very good" yet that's quite a difficult piece to play.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Thu Aug 06, 2009 3:10 am EDIT- Xpost with Jimbo.

[]:             Quote Post by Raian-kun » Thu Aug 06, 2009 3:19 am Thanks to both of you. Also I agree with you that it's a very good example of the character development I love so much in NGE. One can tell it must be a very difficult piece yet Shinji always underestimates himself and plays down his achievements because he doesn't want to be praised by anyone (except for his father) as he finds it embarrassing/uncomfortable.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Aug 06, 2009 5:28 am So Shinji definitely plays a cello , and not a violin? For some reason I've never been able to keep straight exactly what the instrument he plays is.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Aug 06, 2009 5:38 am To be honest, Shinji doesn't actually play any of the hard parts in the show itself, he skips the majority of the prelude and the second movement is the technical/hard one. That doesn't mean he might not know how to play the entire piece. Yes, he plays a cello . A violin is much smaller and you prop it on your neck under your chin.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Thu Aug 06, 2009 1:17 pm Ornette wrote: To be honest, Shinji doesn't actually play any of the hard parts in the show itself, he skips the majority of the prelude and the second movement is the technical/hard one.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Aug 06, 2009 1:22 pm Merridian wrote: Yeah, the end of the first movement has some awesome see-sawing bow action going on that you'd see a lot more of in Suite 3.
